title: Greek Chicken Gyro Wraps
description: Lemon-and-oregano marinated chicken, a quick grated-cucumber tzatziki, and a build-your-own bar so everyone rolls their own.

items: Oven chips (they go inside the wrap)

steps: Marinate first, then prep everything else while it sits. Mix the garlic, olive oil, lemon, oregano, paprika and salt. Cut the thighs into strips — more surface area means the 20 minutes does more work — then coat and set aside.
Make the tzatziki. Grate the cucumber, wring the water out in a tea towel, then stir it into the yoghurt with the garlic and a pinch of salt. Into the fridge.
Prep the fillings. Slice the tomato and onion, shred the lettuce, grate cheese if using. Lay it all out bar-style.
Cook hot and fast. Get the pan properly hot. The strips need just 3–4 minutes, tossing, until charred at the edges and no pink inside. Don't crowd the pan.
Warm the wraps for a few seconds in the dry pan so they fold without cracking.
Lift the short marinade — a squeeze of fresh lemon and a pinch of oregano over the cooked chicken makes up for the quick soak.

Build your own: warm wrap laid flat, a smear of tzatziki down the middle (not the edges, or it squeezes out), chicken strips down the centre, chips if you're going full gyro, then salad and a little onion. Fold one end up first, then roll tight and wrap the base so nothing drops out.
notes: Kids' jobs: grating and wringing the cucumber, mixing the tzatziki, and running the build-your-own line — same appeal as a taco bar. No time to pre-marinate? Cutting the chicken into strips lets 20 minutes do real work, and with strips you can skip resting the meat too. One 500 g tub of Greek yoghurt covers the tzatziki here and a two-ingredient naan another night.

items.name: Lemons
items.hint: 1 juiced for the marinade, wedges to serve

items.name: Red onion
items.hint: recipe uses ¼, but they don't sell quarters

items.name: Souvlaki bread or wraps
items.hint: the medium one — not the thin pocket pita

items.name: Greek yoghurt, thick
items.hint: need 1 cup — tub also covers a naan another night

items.name: Oven chips
items.hint: if going full gyro — chips go inside the wrap

title: Fettuccine Alfredo
description: Silky, restaurant-style alfredo. The sauce comes together in about the time the pasta cooks.
ingredients: 400 g fettuccine (dried is fine) — use ~330 g if scaling the sauce down
85 g unsalted butter (about ⅓ of a 250 g block)
200–240 ml cream (thickened or pure cream is easiest)
100 g Parmigiano-Reggiano, finely grated — plus extra for serving
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Optional: 2 garlic cloves (minced), a pinch of nutmeg
steps: Boil the pasta. Big pot of well-salted water. Cook the fettuccine until just shy of al dente (~12 min is normal). Before draining, scoop out a mug of the starchy pasta water.
Melt the butter in a wide pan on low heat. (Add the garlic now if using — about 1 minute, don't let it brown.)
Pour the cream into the melted butter and heat gently until steaming, not boiling.
Cheese goes in off the heat. Pull the pan off the burner and let it stop bubbling, then add the parmesan a handful at a time, whisking each lot in smoothly before the next.
Drain the pasta (don't rinse) and tip it into the sauce. Toss hard, splashing in pasta water bit by bit until the sauce turns silky and coats every strand. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately on warm plates with extra parmesan grated over the top. Alfredo stiffens as it sits, so don't let it wait.
notes: The rules that matter most: keep the heat low (heat is what breaks the sauce); add the cheese off the heat (too hot turns it grainy and stringy); pasta water is your fix — if it ever looks tight, oily or split, a splash plus hard tossing pulls it back together; and use a real block of parmesan grated yourself, since pre-shredded has anti-caking starch that won't melt cleanly. To level up later, drop the cream entirely and make the Roman version: just butter, parmesan and pasta water emulsified together.

items.name: Parmigiano-Reggiano
items.hint: a real block to grate — not pre-shredded
